meaning: n.内在,固有,[宗](上帝的)无所不在(基督教认为上帝普遍存在于宇宙之中)
examples: And this garb was a perfectly transparent sheath, through which he recognized the glory of the Divine Immanence.
It's not a promise of infinite and lacerating repetitions: it's what makes moments caught up in the immanence of return suddenly appear as ends.
The temples of Buddhism sheltered only a lucky few, and besides, the Buddhist meditative tradition pursues a selfless emptiness, a still point, the harking of universal immanence.
